title: Annual Return Filing Online in India | Consultant, Fees & Service

hero.headline: Company & LLP Annual Return Filing Made Easy
hero.subtext: Ensure legal compliance under Companies Act & LLP Act with Professionals Law’s expert-managed return filings.

mainHeading: Annual Return Filing Online Service in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: Annual Return filing is mandatory for all the companies and LLPs incorporated in India under the laws of Companies Act, 2013 and LLP Act, 2008. These filings require companies and LLPs to disclose vital information with regard to their financials, shareholding, management, economic and statutory compliances, etc., to the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA). Professionals Law provide an end to the end solution for filing your annual return so that you and your business comply with your obligations accurately and in a timely manner.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: This filing of annual return is a mandatory requirement for every company as per Companies Act, 2013 and Limited Liability Act, 2008 and the company has to submit the annual return within 60 days from the date of its AGM (Annual General Meeting). It helps the business to comply with laws and at the same time, are transparent and consistent with corporate governance principles. It is important that businesses meet their Responsibility to file on time and accurately, to prevent penalties, disqualification of directors and potentially prosecution and to ensure responsible business compliance and longer-term credibility. With Professionals Law on your side, saving time and staying compliant is not an issue whenever annual statutory filings are due, without any surprise penalties or law suits that might follow you.

body.title: Legal Continuity & Good Standing
body.description: Timely filing ensures companies and LLPs maintain their active legal status and good corporate standing.

body.title: Avoidance of Penalties
body.description: Late or non-filing attracts substantial penalties; timely filing prevents unnecessary financial burdens.

body.title: Transparency for Stakeholders
body.description: Annual returns keep stakeholders, investors and regulators informed about the company's operations and financial health.

body.title: Builds Business Reputation
body.description: Regular compliance enhances credibility and trust with banks, investors and business partners.

categories.applicantType: The following entities are required to file Annual Returns:
criteria: Note: Even companies or LLPs with no business activity must file NIL returns annually.
With Professionals Law, whether active or dormant, your business remains 100% compliant.

documents: PAN and TAN of the company
Financial Statements (Balance Sheet, P&L Account, Audit Report)
List of Directors, Shareholders and KMPs

body.step: Consultation with Professionals Law compliance experts
Collection and verification of financial data and statutory records
Preparation of necessary forms (MGT-7/7A, AOC-4 for companies; LLP Form-8 & Form-11 for LLPs)
Review and validation of forms and attachments
Filing of Annual Return with MCA portal using DSC

body.title: Companies
body.description: ₹100 per day for each form not filed.

body.title: LLPs
body.description: Maximum 15 times of regular filing fees plus 10 per day for delay beyond 360 days (for small LLPs) and maximum 30 times of regular filing fees plus 20 per day for delay beyond 360 days (for Other than Small LLPs).

body.title: Non-compliance
body.description: May lead to disqualification of directors, prosecution and removal of company name from MCA records.

body.question: Who is required to file Annual Return?
body.answer: All companies and LLPs registered under Companies Act, 2013 or LLP Act, 2008 must file annual returns.

body.question: Is annual filing mandatory even if there is no business activity?
body.answer: Yes, even dormant or non-operational companies/LLPs must file annual returns.

body.question: What forms are required for company annual filings?
body.answer: Companies must file MGT-7A for Annual Return and AOC-4 for Financial Statements.

body.question: What forms are required for LLP annual filings?
body.answer: LLPs must file Form-8 (Statement of Accounts) and Form-11 (Annual Return).

body.question: What are the consequences of non-filing?
body.answer: Heavy penalties, director disqualification and even striking off of the company name.

body.question: Can Annual Return filing be revised after submission?
body.answer: Only limited corrections are permitted; hence, accurate filing is essential.

body.question: What financial details are needed?
body.answer: Balance Sheet, Profit & Loss Account, audit reports and details of shareholding or partners.

body.question: Does Professionals Law assist in annual compliance management?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law provides complete annual compliance management, including filing, advisory and post-filing support.

body.question: Can Professionals Law handle filings for businesses across India?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law serves clients PAN-India through its network of experienced professionals.
